title: 01 Audit and Research Question
thesis: This chapter diagnoses the prior supplement and states the research question in evidence-first terms.
figureCaption: Structured audit table mapping diagnosed weaknesses to specific revision actions.
cautionNote: The goal is sharper evidence communication, not claim inflation.

title: 02 Problem Intuition and Interpretation Rules
thesis: Persistent loops are useful boundary signals, but bridge interpretation requires community-aware cycle logic.
figureCaption: Interpretation checklist and conceptual constraints for reading loop evidence.
cautionNote: A loop is descriptive structure, not direct proof of future topic formation.

title: 03 Data and Yearly Construction
thesis: Yearly analysis is recomputed under a strict historical cutoff so validation remains temporally honest.
figureCaption: Figure 1 pipeline with explicit mathematical objects at each stage.
cautionNote: Cross-year comparison is between yearly states, not one preserved object identity.

title: 04 Mathematical Pipeline
thesis: Each equation is presented with operational interpretation so reviewers can map symbols to code-level objects.
figureCaption: Figure 2 loop birth/death mechanics with fixed node positions and explicit birth edge.
cautionNote: Equations describe computation, not causal mechanism.

title: 05 From Loop to Bridge Candidate
thesis: Bridge candidates are extracted by a precise rule: cross-community edges on representative cycles.
cautionNote: Candidates are hypotheses for review, not guaranteed bridges.

title: 06 Ranking and Temporal Validation
thesis: nDCG@50 evaluates whether later-relevant candidates are concentrated near the top of the ranked list.
figureCaption: Figure 4 ranking logic with toy nDCG example.
cautionNote: Validation is retrospective on held-out future years.

title: 07 Evidence Walkthroughs
thesis: Case walkthroughs connect geometry, loop structure, community boundary, and later relevance evidence.
cautionNote: Case narratives should be interpreted with explicit claim boundaries.

title: 08 Claim Boundaries
thesis: This chapter separates direct evidence, indirect support, and unsupported extrapolation.
cautionNote: Avoid sliding from structure description into untested causal statements.

title: 09 Reference Integrity and Figure Specs
thesis: Methodological references are metadata-checked and tied to explicit methodological claims.
figureCaption: Reference audit table and implementation-ready figure specifications.
cautionNote: Insert final archival metadata for the CHI 2026 manuscript when available.
